title: Add --isolation rootless
body:
Add an isolation mode which also calls an OCI runtime, but with modified arguments, passing it a modified runtime configuration.  This mode does things which are currently not permitted for containers in SELinux policy.
We now reexec ourselves in a new user namespace — even when we're running with UID 0 — if we don't have `CAP_SYS_ADMIN`, so that we can use `unshare(2)` when setting up to run a runtime.

comments.body: @nalind What AVCs are you seeing?

DOes this replace https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/836 or in addition?
@giuseppe PTAL

My preference would be to leave the SELinux policy alone, as that means we don't grant more privileges to containers, but these are logged in permissive mode:
```
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.684:21985): avc:  denied  { mount } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="/" dev="devpts" ino=1 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:devpts_t:s0 tclass=filesystem permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.684:21984): avc:  denied  { mount } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="/" dev="tmpfs" ino=20748791 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:tmpfs_t:s0 tclass=filesystem permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.684:21986): avc:  denied  { mounton } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" path="/tmp/buildah730234659/mnt/rootfs/dev/pts" dev="devpts" ino=1 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:devpts_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.684:21987): avc:  denied  { mounton } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" path="/tmp/buildah730234659/mnt/rootfs/proc" dev="proc" ino=1 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:proc_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.684:21988): avc:  denied  { remount } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:sysfs_t:s0 tclass=filesystem permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.685:21989): avc:  denied  { open } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" path="/dev/pts/ptmx" dev="devpts" ino=2 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:devpts_t:s0 tclass=chr_file permissive=1
----
time->Thu Jul 19 15:56:17 2018
type=AVC msg=audit(1532030177.700:21990): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=13547 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" name="0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c508,c877 tcontext=system_u:object_r:devpts_t:s0 tclass=chr_file permissive=1
----
```

These denials are seen when running `buildah run` inside of a container.  The /dev, /proc, and /sys mountpoints start off as ordinary directories in the rootfs that buildah has been building.  The devpts filesystem is being mounted with the "newinstance" flag.
Is buildah passing in the mount label of the container it is running in?  I think the issue might be that we are not passing down the label so buildah a devpts which is not labeled correctly?
As near as the `buildah` process can tell, SELinux is disabled, and `github.com/opencontainers/selinux/go-selinux/label.InitLabels()` returns empty values for it to use.
Just for grins could you hard code a label into mountlabel, to see if the AVC's get better?

When setting processLabel to `system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c26,c85` and mountLabel to `system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c26,c85`, runc's validator logic returns an `selinux label is specified in config, but selinux is disabled or not supported` error.
Ok We will have to think of planb.  I had a patch for selinux to allow the applying of labels even if selinux is disabled, but maybe that will not work.

Happy green test buttons.  I'm fine with addressing my comments in a follow up if they're pertinent.

The `MoreCreateArgs` value is passed to the child [here](https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/873/files#diff-12f3273da1625fbe0b8ae7f4eefe2c3cR1297), so that its values can be used as additional command line arguments for the runtime's `create` command.  Arguments that specify where to send the console socket are appended to the passed-in value instead of its previous empty default value.
Yeah I saw the MoreCreateArgs getting passed, I just didn't find any code that actually used it once passed. I may have missed it or have older code.
